annotations.label: Erdős number (collaborative distance)

anchor.snippet: between mathematician Paul Erdős and another person, measured by joint authorship

annotations.note: The collaborative distance is the shortest-walk distance from a fixed vertex in the coauthorship graph, which `SimpleGraph.dist` generalizes, though no Erdős-specific definition exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Median Erdős number of Fields Medalists is 3

anchor.snippet: The median Erdős number of Fields Medalists is 3

annotations.note: This is an empirical statistic about real-world coauthorship and is not a formalizable mathematical statement.

annotations.label: About 5% have Erdős number ≤ 2

anchor.snippet: have an Erdős number of 2 or lower

annotations.note: An empirical demographic statistic with no Mathlib counterpart.

annotations.label: Lowest achievable Erdős number increases over time

anchor.snippet: the lowest Erdős number that can still be achieved will necessarily increase

annotations.note: A historical/temporal observation about real-world collaboration, not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ramanujan has Erdős number 3

anchor.snippet: Srinivasa Ramanujan has an Erdős number of only 3

annotations.note: A fact about a specific person's coauthorship distance, not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Definition of Erdős number (coauthorship chain)

anchor.snippet: someone must be a coauthor of a research paper with another person who has a finite Erdős number

annotations.note: The inductive coauthorship-chain definition is the graph-distance notion generalized by `SimpleGraph.dist`, but the collaboration graph itself is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 509 direct collaborators have Erdős number 1

anchor.snippet: He had 509 direct collaborators

annotations.note: An empirical count of Erdős's coauthors, not a Mathlib statement.

annotations.label: Lowest new Erdős number is 2 since Erdős's death

anchor.snippet: the lowest Erdős number that a new researcher can obtain is 2

annotations.note: A real-world observation contingent on Erdős's death, not formalizable in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Erdős number of the second kind

anchor.snippet: restricts assignment of Erdős numbers to papers with only two collaborators

annotations.note: This variant is still graph distance in a restricted coauthorship graph, generalized by `SimpleGraph.dist`, but the specific construction is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Earliest people with finite Erdős number

anchor.snippet: The earliest person known to have a finite Erdős number is either Antoine Lavoisier

annotations.label: Monotone Erdős number (directed graph)

anchor.snippet: proposed a directed graph version of the Erdős number problem

annotations.note: Mathlib's `SimpleGraph.dist` is for undirected graphs only and has no directed/monotone-distance analogue formalized.

annotations.label: Rational Erdős numbers (resistor network)

anchor.snippet: form an electrical network with a one-ohm resistor on each edge

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize electrical networks or effective resistance, so this resistor-distance notion is absent.

annotations.label: Tozier auctioned an Erdős number 5

anchor.snippet: auctioned off a co-authorship on eBay , hence providing the buyer with an Erdős number of 5

annotations.note: A real-world anecdote, not a formalizable mathematical statement.

annotations.label: Bacon number

anchor.snippet: connecting actors to the actor Kevin Bacon by a chain of joint appearances in films

annotations.note: The Bacon number is graph distance from a fixed vertex in the co-appearance graph, generalized by `SimpleGraph.dist`, but no actor-specific definition exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Erdős–Bacon number

anchor.snippet: thus have an Erdős–Bacon number , the sum of the two

annotations.note: This ad-hoc sum of two distinct graph distances is not a defined concept in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Danica McKellar has Erdős–Bacon number 6

anchor.snippet: has an Erdős-Bacon number of 6

annotations.note: A fact about a specific person, not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Erdős–Bacon–Sabbath number

anchor.snippet: is the sum of the Erdős–Bacon number and the collaborative distance to the band Black Sabbath

annotations.note: A composite sum of three distances with no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Morphy number

anchor.snippet: the Morphy number measures a player's connection to Paul Morphy

annotations.note: The Morphy number is graph distance from a fixed vertex in a chess-opponent graph, generalized by `SimpleGraph.dist`, but the specific graph is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Shusaku number

anchor.snippet: the Shusaku number measures a player's connection to Hon'inbō Shūsaku

annotations.note: The Shusaku number is graph distance from a fixed vertex in a Go-opponent graph, generalized by `SimpleGraph.dist`, but no such definition exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ryu number

anchor.snippet: the Ryu number measures a video game character's connection to the Street Fighter character Ryu

annotations.note: The Ryu number is graph distance from a fixed vertex in a character co-appearance graph, generalized by `SimpleGraph.dist`, but the specific graph is not in Mathlib.
